speaker: ANTONY
text: Eros, thou yet behold’st me?

speaker: EROS
text: Ay, noble lord.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Sometime we see a cloud that’s dragonish,

speaker: ANTONY
text: A vapor sometime like a bear or lion,

speaker: ANTONY
text: A towered citadel, a pendent rock,

speaker: ANTONY
text: A forkèd mountain, or blue promontory

speaker: ANTONY
text: With trees upon ’t that nod unto the world

speaker: ANTONY
text: And mock our eyes with air. Thou hast seen these signs.

speaker: ANTONY
text: They are black vesper’s pageants.

speaker: EROS
text: Ay, my lord.

speaker: ANTONY
text: That which is now a horse, even with a thought

speaker: ANTONY
text: The rack dislimns and makes it indistinct

speaker: ANTONY
text: As water is in water.

speaker: EROS
text: It does, my lord.

speaker: ANTONY
text: My good knave Eros, now thy captain is

speaker: ANTONY
text: Even such a body. Here I am Antony,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Yet cannot hold this visible shape, my knave.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I made these wars for Egypt, and the Queen,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Whose heart I thought I had, for she had mine—

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which whilst it was mine had annexed unto ’t

speaker: ANTONY
text: A million more, now lost—she, Eros, has

speaker: ANTONY
text: Packed cards with Caesar and false-played my glory

speaker: ANTONY
text: Unto an enemy’s triumph.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Nay, weep not, gentle Eros. There is left us

speaker: ANTONY
text: Ourselves to end ourselves.

speaker: ANTONY
text: O, thy vile lady!

speaker: ANTONY
text: She has robbed me of my sword.

speaker: MARDIAN
text: No, Antony,

speaker: MARDIAN
text: My mistress loved thee and her fortunes mingled

speaker: MARDIAN
text: With thine entirely.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Hence, saucy eunuch! Peace!

speaker: ANTONY
text: She hath betrayed me and shall die the death.

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Death of one person can be paid but once,

speaker: MARDIAN
text: And that she has discharged. What thou wouldst do

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Is done unto thy hand. The last she spake

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Was “Antony, most noble Antony.”

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Then in the midst a tearing groan did break

speaker: MARDIAN
text: The name of Antony; it was divided

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Between her heart and lips. She rendered life

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Thy name so buried in her.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Dead, then?

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Dead.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Unarm, Eros. The long day’s task is done,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And we must sleep.—That thou depart’st hence safe

speaker: ANTONY
text: Does pay thy labor richly. Go.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Off, pluck off!

speaker: ANTONY
text: The sevenfold shield of Ajax cannot keep

speaker: ANTONY
text: The battery from my heart. O, cleave, my sides!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Heart, once be stronger than thy continent;

speaker: ANTONY
text: Crack thy frail case. Apace, Eros, apace!

speaker: ANTONY
text: No more a soldier. Bruisèd pieces, go.

speaker: ANTONY
text: You have been nobly borne.—From me awhile.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I will o’ertake thee, Cleopatra, and

speaker: ANTONY
text: Weep for my pardon. So it must be, for now

speaker: ANTONY
text: All length is torture. Since the torch is out,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Lie down and stray no farther. Now all labor

speaker: ANTONY
text: Mars what it does; yea, very force entangles

speaker: ANTONY
text: Itself with strength. Seal, then, and all is done.—

speaker: ANTONY
text: Eros!—I come, my queen.—Eros!—Stay for me.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Where souls do couch on flowers, we’ll hand in hand,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And with our sprightly port make the ghosts gaze.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Dido and her Aeneas shall want troops,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And all the haunt be ours.—Come, Eros, Eros!

speaker: EROS
text: What would my lord?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Since Cleopatra died

speaker: ANTONY
text: I have lived in such dishonor that the gods

speaker: ANTONY
text: Detest my baseness. I, that with my sword

speaker: ANTONY
text: Quartered the world and o’er green Neptune’s back

speaker: ANTONY
text: With ships made cities, condemn myself to lack

speaker: ANTONY
text: The courage of a woman—less noble mind

speaker: ANTONY
text: Than she which, by her death, our Caesar tells

speaker: ANTONY
text: “I am conqueror of myself.” Thou art sworn, Eros,

speaker: ANTONY
text: That when the exigent should come, which now

speaker: ANTONY
text: Is come indeed, when I should see behind me

speaker: ANTONY
text: Th’ inevitable prosecution of

speaker: ANTONY
text: Disgrace and horror, that on my command

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thou then wouldst kill me. Do ’t. The time is come.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thou strik’st not me; ’tis Caesar thou defeat’st.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Put color in thy cheek.

speaker: EROS
text: The gods withhold me!

speaker: EROS
text: Shall I do that which all the Parthian darts,

speaker: EROS
text: Though enemy, lost aim and could not?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Eros,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Wouldst thou be windowed in great Rome and see

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thy master thus with pleached arms, bending down

speaker: ANTONY
text: His corrigible neck, his face subdued

speaker: ANTONY
text: To penetrative shame, whilst the wheeled seat

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of fortunate Caesar, drawn before him, branded

speaker: ANTONY
text: His baseness that ensued?

speaker: EROS
text: I would not see ’t.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Come, then, for with a wound I must be cured.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Draw that thy honest sword, which thou hast worn

speaker: ANTONY
text: Most useful for thy country.

speaker: EROS
text: O, sir, pardon me!

speaker: ANTONY
text: When I did make thee free, swor’st thou not then

speaker: ANTONY
text: To do this when I bade thee? Do it at once,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Or thy precedent services are all

speaker: ANTONY
text: But accidents unpurposed. Draw, and come.

speaker: EROS
text: Turn from me then that noble countenance

speaker: EROS
text: Wherein the worship of the whole world lies.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Lo thee!

speaker: EROS
text: My sword is drawn.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Then let it do at once

speaker: ANTONY
text: The thing why thou hast drawn it.

speaker: EROS
text: My dear master,

speaker: EROS
text: My captain, and my emperor, let me say,

speaker: EROS
text: Before I strike this bloody stroke, farewell.

speaker: ANTONY
text: ’Tis said, man, and farewell.

speaker: EROS
text: Farewell, great chief. Shall I strike now?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Now, Eros.

speaker: EROS
text: Why, there, then.

speaker: EROS
text: Thus I do escape the sorrow

speaker: EROS
text: Of Antony’s death.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thrice nobler than myself,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thou teachest me, O valiant Eros, what

speaker: ANTONY
text: I should and thou couldst not. My queen and Eros

speaker: ANTONY
text: Have by their brave instruction got upon me

speaker: ANTONY
text: A nobleness in record. But I will be

speaker: ANTONY
text: A bridegroom in my death and run into ’t

speaker: ANTONY
text: As to a lover’s bed. Come then, and, Eros,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thy master dies thy scholar. To do thus

speaker: ANTONY
text:
I learned of thee.
                 
                He
                 
                stabs
                 
                himself
                .
               How, not dead? Not dead?

speaker: ANTONY
text: The guard, ho! O, dispatch me!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: What’s the noise?

speaker: ANTONY
text: I have done my work ill, friends. O, make an end

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of what I have begun!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GUARD
text: The star is fall’n.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: And time is at his period.

speaker: ALL
text: Alas, and woe!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let him that loves me strike me dead.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: Not I.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GUARD
text: Nor I.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              GUARD
text: Nor anyone.

speaker: DERCETUS
text: Thy death and fortunes bid thy followers fly.

speaker: DERCETUS
text: This sword but shown to Caesar with this tidings

speaker: DERCETUS
text: Shall enter me with him.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Where’s Antony?

speaker: DERCETUS
text: There, Diomed, there.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Lives he? Wilt thou not answer, man?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Art thou there, Diomed? Draw thy sword, and give me

speaker: ANTONY
text: Sufficing strokes for death.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Most absolute lord,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: My mistress Cleopatra sent me to thee.

speaker: ANTONY
text: When did she send thee?

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Now, my lord.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Where is she?

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Locked in her monument. She had a prophesying fear

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Of what hath come to pass. For when she saw—

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Which never shall be found—you did suspect

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: She had disposed with Caesar, and that your rage

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Would not be purged, she sent you word she was dead;

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: But fearing since how it might work, hath sent

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Me to proclaim the truth, and I am come,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: I dread, too late.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Too late, good Diomed. Call my guard, I prithee.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: What ho! The Emperor’s guard! The guard, what ho!

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Come, your lord calls.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Bear me, good friends, where Cleopatra bides.

speaker: ANTONY
text: ’Tis the last service that I shall command you.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: Woe, woe are we, sir, you may not live to wear

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: All your true followers out.

speaker: ALL
text: Most heavy day!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Nay, good my fellows, do not please sharp fate

speaker: ANTONY
text: To grace it with your sorrows. Bid that welcome

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which comes to punish us, and we punish it,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Seeming to bear it lightly. Take me up.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I have led you oft; carry me now, good friends,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And have my thanks for all.
